subject: Social Science
overall_learning_objectives: Analyze historical and contemporary challenges faced by women
Investigate contributions of women change-makers in India and globally
Develop research, critical thinking, and analytical skills
Conduct surveys and interviews to understand societal attitudes
Encourage civic awareness, advocacy, and presentation skills

lesson_plan.title: Historical Challenges for Women
topics_covered: Social, cultural, and educational restrictions on women
student_activities: Research one historical challenge faced by women
Write a short explanation connecting it to societal norms

lesson_plan.title: Women Change-Makers
topics_covered: Key figures: Ramabai, Rokeya, Rashsundari Devi, and others
teaching_strategy: Use slides, videos, or printed biographies
student_activities: Prepare a comparative table of 3 women leaders

lesson_plan.title: Analyzing Gender Stereotypes
topics_covered: Common stereotypes about men and women in society
Ways to challenge and change stereotypes
teaching_strategy: Group discussion with examples from media and community
student_activities: Conduct a mini survey in class or at home
Collect examples of stereotypes and their impact
Prepare a short report summarizing findings

lesson_plan.title: Research and Reflection: Women Today
topics_covered: Current roles of women in society
Opportunities in education, professions, and leadership
student_activities: Research 2–3 contemporary women leaders in your community or media
Write a 1-page reflection comparing their opportunities with women in the past

lesson_plan.title: Debate and Policy Proposal
topics_covered: Contemporary gender issues (education, wage gap, safety, domestic work)
student_activities: Prepare arguments for or against a given gender issue
Draft a small policy proposal or campaign plan

lesson_plan.title: Project Presentation and Reflection
topics_covered: Present findings from surveys, research, and case studies
Reflect on lessons learned from women’s achievements
Discuss how students can promote equality in school and community
student_activities: Present survey results, research, or creative projects
Write a final reflection on key lessons learned
Discuss ways to implement ideas for equality in daily life

teaching_aids: Pictures, biographies, and videos of women leaders
Charts for data collection and analysis
Slides or printed mini case studies
teacher_tip: Encourage students to connect historical struggles with current issues. Assign research, surveys, debates, and presentations. Promote discussion, reflection, and civic awareness to deepen understanding of gender equality.
